meta.appDescription: Browser-based planner for household cash flow, rentals, and long-range savings curves—with scenario compare. Free; optional Google sign-in to save scenarios on the server.

siteFooter.tagline: Guides and simulator: editorial content and tooling built for Finance Pilot.
siteFooter.aboutEditorial: Guides and information pages are written for Finance Pilot by the site author.
siteFooter.privacyLocal: When signed in with Google, your simulator scenarios are stored on our servers; without an account they only exist in the browser session until you close the tab.
siteFooter.cookiesNote: Anonymous audience metrics may be used to improve the site; we do not sell advertising profiles.

navigation.themeCycle: Theme: tap to cycle system, light, then dark

auth.bannerGuestDescription: You can enter and edit everything freely. To save your scenarios, named projects, and monthly checklist on the server, sign in with Google.
auth.bannerSynced: Your simulator data is saved to your signed-in account (automatic sync).
auth.bannerBackendDisabled: Cloud saving is not enabled on this deployment (OAuth / database env vars missing). The simulator still works for the current browser session.

homePage.metaTitle: Finance Pilot: household budget, cash flow and wealth simulator (free)
homePage.metaDescription: Plan household money flows: data entry, monthly calendar, long-range charts and scenario compare. Educational guides plus a browser-based tool, no mandatory account.
schemaFeatureList: Site-written guides: steps, budgeting, wealth strategies
Income, bills, envelopes and investments in one workspace
Month view for due dates and paid/not-paid tracking
Draft vs saved named projects; local JSON backup/import
homePage.heroTitle: See your budget and wealth path more clearly
homePage.heroLead: Guides written for this site to frame your choices, then a simulator to run the numbers: flows, month by month, projections and comparisons—your data stays on your device.
homePage.privacyText: Without an account, the simulator runs in your browser session. With Google, named projects and the monthly checklist are saved on our servers. JSON export/import remains available. Switch language (French or English) from the header.

featureCards.title: Data entry
featureCards.description: Enter wages or variable pay, rent received, loan payments, sinking funds (travel, car), rentals, and holdings—the sheet updates cash you could still deploy.

featureCards.title: Monthly management
featureCards.description: A payment calendar, checkboxes when items clear, and a category split so you quickly spot drift from the plan.

featureCards.title: Forecasts
featureCards.description: Long-range curves from your assumed average return and contributions—useful to visualize discipline, not a guarantee of future performance.

featureCards.title: Compare
featureCards.description: Line up two versions (draft vs saved project): balances, flows, and savings curves to pick which strategy feels right.

homePage.guidesSectionLead: Browse a guide below, then open the simulator to run the numbers—everything stays on this home page.

guideCards.title: Manage your money in four steps
guideCards.description: A simple frame: know your flows, set priorities, automate savings, and review every month.

guideCards.title: Seven money-management strategies
guideCards.description: A tour of common approaches (50/30/20, pay yourself first, FIRE, property…) and who they tend to fit.

guideCards.title: Which strategies fit your profile?
guideCards.description: Five questions: several guide sections may be suggested when scores stay close, with links to each (educational pointer only).

guideCards.title: Monthly budget and cash flow
guideCards.description: Anticipate outflows, read your cash position, and avoid surprises with calendars and sinking funds.

guideCards.title: Budgeting and saving: practical methods
guideCards.description: 50/30/20, automated savings, envelope budgeting, emergency funds, and debt payoff strategies—frame choices before you model them in the app.

simulateurPage.heroLead: Pick a module to enter your figures, track the current month, or compare assumptions.

featureCards.title: Data entry
featureCards.description: Enter wages or variable pay, rent received, loan payments, sinking funds (travel, car), rentals, and holdings—the sheet updates cash you could still deploy.

featureCards.title: Monthly management
featureCards.description: A payment calendar, checkboxes when items clear, and a category split so you quickly spot drift from the plan.

featureCards.title: Forecasts
featureCards.description: Long-range curves from your assumed average return and contributions—useful to visualize discipline, not a guarantee of future performance.

featureCards.title: Compare
featureCards.description: Line up two versions (draft vs saved project): balances, flows, and savings curves to pick which strategy feels right.

simulateur.title: Open the simulator: data, monthly management, forecasts and compare
simulateur.description: Entry point to Finance Pilot modules: cash-flow inputs, monthly calendar, long-range curves and side-by-side analysis. Local-first in your browser.

donnees.title: Income, bills, and holdings in one household cash-flow workspace
donnees.description: One screen for household inflows, rentals, and savings inputs—cash available to invest updates as you type. JSON backup stays in the browser; import restores a prior snapshot.

gestionFinances.title: Monthly calendar: due dates, debits, and category split
gestionFinances.description: See what should leave the account each month, mark items paid, and review category shares to catch recurring costs that creep upward.

estimations.title: Savings and portfolio curves over 10–30 years
estimations.description: Tune contributions, average return, and horizon—the app plots an indicative capital path. Helps visualize time in market and assumptions, not a promise of returns.

comparaison.title: Compare two plans: draft vs saved projects
comparaison.description: Align metrics, flows, and savings curves to choose which version of your roadmap to keep—handy before a life change or portfolio shift.

strategiesPatrimoine.title: Budgeting and saving: 50/30/20, envelopes, emergency fund, and debt payoff
strategiesPatrimoine.description: A practical household budgeting guide: the 50/30/20 rule, pay yourself first, envelope budgeting, emergency funds, debt snowball vs avalanche, and horizon goals—so you can frame choices before modelling them in Finance Pilot.

guides.title: Personal finance guides: budgeting, cash flow and methods
guides.description: Guides written for Finance Pilot to structure your money (steps, habits, monthly budgets), then test it with the local simulator, monthly management, and projections.

guidesFinances4Etapes.title: Manage your money in four steps: a simple, practical frame
guidesFinances4Etapes.description: Four moves from fuzzy to planned: know your flows, set priorities, automate savings and review monthly—before you enter numbers in the simulator.

guidesSeptStrategies.title: Seven money-management strategies: levers and trade-offs
guidesSeptStrategies.description: A detailed Finance Pilot guide: 50/30/20, pay yourself first, FIRE, core/satellite portfolios, the “100 minus age” rule, property—fit by profile, pros, cons, and how to model it in the simulator.

guidesBudgetTresorerie.title: Monthly budget and cash flow: stay ahead of bills
guidesBudgetTresorerie.description: How to read cash flow, anticipate outflows and avoid overdrafts: bill calendars, sinking funds and useful indicators—linked to Data and Monthly management modules.

guidesStrategieProfil.title: Which money-management strategies fit your profile?
guidesStrategieProfil.description: Five-question quiz scoring seven approaches from the strategies guide; several sections may be suggested when scores stay close, with links into the guide.

savedProjects.autoSaveDraft: Draft: load a project to attach changes to a named snapshot.
savedProjects.draftSessionOnly: Session draft: changes are not stored on the server until you sign in.
savedProjects.loginToSaveHint: Google sign-in is required to create named projects and save them to the database.
savedProjects.oauthNotConfigured: Google OAuth is not configured on the server or client app: cloud save is unavailable for now.

dataPage.title: Data entry
dataPage.subtitle: Enter your income, spending, rental properties, and investments.
dataPage.footer: Without an account, your draft lives in the browser session. Sign in with Google to save to the server via “Projects”. JSON export/import below.

dataJson.confirmImport: Replace all on-screen data with this file? Current data will be lost unless you exported it elsewhere.

error.invalid_json: This file is not valid JSON (syntax error).
error.not_object: JSON must be an object { … }, not an array or plain text.
error.unexpected_shape: This JSON does not look like a Finance Pilot backup (expected keys such as revenues, fixed expenses, annex budgets, etc.).

rental.title: Rental properties
rental.maintenanceHintPct: % of rent set aside each month for upkeep
rental.maintenanceHintFixed: Fixed amount set aside for upkeep
rental.otherChargesHint: Fees, agent, accountant, external management, etc. — in euros (monthly or yearly) or as a % of monthly rent received.

investments.title: Investments and savings
investments.placeholder: E.g. stocks & shares ISA, crypto, bond…

estimations.title: Forecasts and projections
estimations.subtitle: See your finances at a glance and project your wealth over time.
estimations.noInvestmentsBody: Add investments on the Data page to see how your portfolio could grow over time.
estimations.footerDisclaimer: Forecasts use the returns you enter; they are not a guarantee of future performance.

comparaison.title: Compare projects
comparaison.subtitle: Compare projected performance over several years: portfolio value from investments (same method as the Forecasts page), alongside monthly balances and net rental income for each scenario.
comparaison.alertNoProjectsBody: You can compare the draft to itself to try the screen, or save projects from the “Projects” menu to compare stored scenarios.
comparaison.alertSameBody: Both lines use the same data. Pick two different sources for a meaningful comparison.
comparaison.scenarioADesc: Data used for the first line
comparaison.scenarioBDesc: Data used for the second line
comparaison.projectionDesc: Estimated value of investment lines (contributions and growth at the rate entered for each line).
comparaison.noInvestmentsBoth: Neither scenario has investments: add investment lines on the Data page to see a projection.
comparaison.gapHint: Positive if scenario A is ahead of B.
comparaison.tableDesc: Portfolio value and gap (A − B) for each year in the horizon.
comparaison.footerDisclaimer: Illustrative projections only: they use the returns you entered and do not predict real markets.

schedule.hint: For monthly tracking: category, mode, and day.
schedule.categoryNoMatch: No saved category matches. Type a name to create it below.
schedule.manual: Manual (transfer or payment you must initiate)
schedule.automatic: Automatic (direct debit on a set day)

advancedFinance.title: Monthly cashflow
advancedFinance.subtitle: As of today: manual transfers, upcoming automatic debits, and spend by category (scheduled fixed charges & annex budgets).
advancedFinance.empty: No lines have scheduling turned on. On the Data page, open a fixed charge or annex budget, tick “Enable monthly scheduling”, pick manual vs automatic and the day of month, then save. If you leave category blank, the line label is used.
advancedFinance.upcomingTitle: Automatic debits still due this month
advancedFinance.upcomingDesc: Debits still due this month (including today if it is the due day).
advancedFinance.manualDesc: One row per scheduling category: the amount is the sum of monthly equivalents. “Details” opens the lines in that group (tick “Done” per line). Checkmarks reset at the start of each month.
advancedFinance.detailNoEntity: This line no longer exists in your data.
advancedFinance.resetMonthConfirm: Clear all “done” checkmarks for the current month?
advancedFinance.pastAutoTitle: Automatic debits already taken this month

errors.financeContext: useFinanceData must be used inside a FinanceDataProvider
